zxing/zxing.body: Looks fine. I think I can lift position++ out of  the switch. I'll do that myself afterwards as I will also press the button to standardize the code style in the test.

Javadoc has to start with /*\* to be treated as javadoc
Deserves a note that negative values mean "no preference", here and in the other method
Tiny style pref -- I'd write this as ">= 0"
Yes, throw IllegalStateException
This can be combined with the existing method, no?
I'm okay with either; what is the etiquette - should you change these little things yourself if accepting, or should I change them and make another request?
Yes; did not leave a note under assumption that it is obvious from definition of cameraId at http://developer.android.com/reference/android/hardware/Camera.html#open(int)
Pardon me, don't get the question. You're showing a constructor I did not touch.

This is the first line of the block you added with a new `open()` method, whose logic is quite similar to the existing one. I imagine this can be written as one method with logic that handles the case of a specific request, or the default.
This and other instances of "> -1" should also be changed to ">= 0"
I hope I've done what you meant.

zxing/zxing.body: The indentation of a lot of your code doesn't match the rest of the source -- 2-space indent
I don't want to drop support for Android 4.0.x (API 15) yet. This won't work since this key isn't defined in earlier SDKs. I think more reflection is needed to make this work.
Hm, so this doesn't work through public APIs? I thought it would be possible, given projects like https://github.com/jzplusplus/GlassWifiConnect/
Sure, can you give this the same treatment as above? or does it not work?
OK looking strong. I think ChecksumException can get the same treatment.
I think this method can work just like `set()`. You complement the `intMask` with `~` and then `&=` it.
Is this not really implementing "xor"?
Add yourself to `AUTHORS`. I am avoiding `@author` tags.
Really minor but I prefer to avoid names starting with _. Just call it `dataMatrixClone` or something.
Indentation and spacing is off here.
Call this "CODE_39_FULL_ASCII" ? IIRC there is another format mode called 'extended' but I can't find it now.

zxing/zxing.body: Looks like this is 4.1+ only: http://developer.android.com/reference/android/net/Uri.html#normalizeScheme()  Right now it's still on 4.0.x. Eventually it'd be fine to up the minimum but I wouldn't do it just for this.

I don't mind efforts to expand TIFF support. How about using JAI though? I'm a little less inclined to add a whole new dependency for it, but if we must, a "standard" Java lib might be preferably especially if it integrations with `ImageIO`.
Hm, JAI is really old, maybe not. I'm OK with a dependency if it's Maven, licensed reasonably, not too bloated. Also can you describe what kinds of TIFFs ImageIO doesn't read?

For the TIFF format, I have read a few forum and they say that you need another plugin (http://stackoverflow.com/questions/1954685/cant-read-and-write-a-tiff-image-file-using-java-imageio-standard-library). I am trying to read some 1-page scans (I am not allowed to show them). The code is only trying to read the first page (maybe as a future modification, the choice can be given to the user). I have looked at JAI but as you said, it is really old and by using ImageJ, a lot of image processing tools are imported (therefore they can be used in order to improve the detection).
For maven, it is my first time using it, I did not realize that you have a repository. I found ImageJ on it, I will add this dependency (and it will solve the other comment). Thank you for your comments.
For the license, the only thing that I found is on wikipedia: http://imagej.net/Licensing (it seems that the license is unclear due to the participation of non us government employee to a government code but it is in the public domain).
I do not know if the code is bloated (I did not looked at the source code) but the zip that you can download on their website is about 60MB.

Yikes, this is a lot of extra code to special case one arg to one encoder. Why not handle this via the existing hint? It wouldn't hurt to let the code downstream parse an String if needed to make this work. No need for an arbitrary mapping to int
Tiny, but let's just use `null` as a default
Nits: use `Map` as the variable type, and omit the generic types after `HashMap`. You're also missing spaces before arguments as in ",hints)" instead of ", hints)"
Oh I just mean write `= new HashMap<>();`

I'm sorry to nit pick, but while we're here, there are a number of style issues. (Some IDEs can help with this.)

Imports are not ordered alphabetically; still missing a space after arg on line 55; line 58 adds an extra indent that should not be there, and the type of `hints` can really be `Map<EncodeHintType,Object>` as it does not in general only map to strings.

ok, It is not a problem, I can understand that.
I am downloading eclipse and correcting everything
Sure, is this mostly a clone of the Code 39 writer? is there any opportunity to refactor and avoid duplication here?
